title: Kafka Streams
learningObjectives: Tune **window** **grace**, **suppress**, and **retention** for late data policies
Operate **Kafka Streams** with disk, **restore**, and **standby** awareness
Contrast **at-least-once** and **exactly-once** processing modes with broker requirements
Use **Interactive Queries** safely with authentication and data classification

sections.title: Why Kafka Streams matters
sections.content:
**Kafka Streams** collapses **stream** processing into **library** form—no separate **cluster** to babysit—but **state**, **partitions**, and **join** constraints still dominate **on-call** stories.

Interviewers expect you to know **changelog**, **restore**, **repartition**, and **grace** trade-offs with numbers.

sections.title: Theory and Internals - Kafka Streams
sections.content:
### Day 63 — **Kafka** **Streams**

### 1. Building blocks
**StreamsBuilder**, **KStream**, **KTable**, **GlobalKTable**.

### 2. Stateful ops
**aggregate**, **reduce**, **join** with **materialized** stores.

### 3. Fault tolerance
**Changelog** + **restore** from Kafka.

### 4. Windows
**Tumbling**, **sliding**, **grace**.

### 5. EOS
**Exactly-once** processing toggle and broker requirements.

### 6. Story
**Restore** storm after retention misconfig → extend retention + **standby**.

sections.title: Basic — Streams vocabulary
sections.output:
KStream: append-only event flow
KTable: compacted changelog view
changelog: durable backing log for state

sections.title: Intermediate — Word split toy
sections.description: Split line to tokens for windowed count mental model.

sections.title: Advanced — Window bucket toy
Stateful processor with changelog
sections.description: Tasks persist to RocksDB and changelog topic.
sections.plantuml:
@startuml
title Day 63 — Kafka Streams state
participant Task
database RocksDB
queue Changelog
Task -> RocksDB : read/write
Task -> Changelog : append updates
Changelog -> Task : restore on restart
@enduml

sections.title: Exercise — Kafka Streams — stateful ops (Day 63 assignment)
hints: Name one **metric** and one **config** you would check on-call for this day’s topic.
State a **failure mode** (e.g. rebalance, ISR shrink, schema break) tied to your answer.

sections.title: Use cases — Kafka Streams
sections.content:
## Use cases — **Kafka Streams**

### 1) **Session** **window** **fraud** **rules**
**Context:** **5-minute** **inactivity** **windows** per **user** **session**.
**Why Day 63:** **Grace** period and **late** **events** change **counts**.
**Implementation outline:**
1. Pick **window** + **grace** from **SLO** and **source** **clock** **skew**.
2. Size **state** **store** **changelog** **replication** for **AZ** loss.
3. Monitor **restore** **time** after **restarts**.
**Failure hook:** **Repartition** **storm** when **key** **schema** **changes**.

### 2) **Inventory** **KTable** **lookup**
**Context:** **Stream** of **orders** **enriched** with **product** **master** **data**.
**Why Day 63:** **Join** **semantics** and **changelog** **compaction**.
**Implementation outline:**
1. Materialize **table** with **correct** **retention** / **cleanup** policy.
2. Plan **repartition** topics for **foreign-key** joins.
3. **IQ** endpoints only with **read** **replicas** / **standbys** for **HA**.
**Failure hook:** **Stale** **join** after **restore** lag.

### 3) **Anomaly** **detection** **aggregate**
**Context:** **Rolling** **sum** of **errors** per **service**.
**Why Day 63:** **State** **size** and **rocksdb** **tuning**.
**Implementation outline:**
1. **Sliding** vs **tumbling** based on **alert** **semantics**.
2. **Suppress** **until** **window** **closes** for **stable** **outputs**.
3. **Chaos** **restart** **streams** app during **load** **test**.
**Failure hook:** **Duplicate** **aggregates** if **EOS** **disabled** during **failover**.

### 4) **Multi-tenant** **fairness**
**Context:** One **cluster**; **noisy** **tenant** can **blow** **state** **size**.
**Why Day 63:** **Topology** **per** **tenant** tier vs **shared** **with** **quotas**.
**Implementation outline:**
1. **Isolate** **hot** **tenants** to **dedicated** **application** **instances**.
2. **Cap** **per-tenant** **keys** in **operator** **review**.
**Failure hook:** **Single** **tenant** **skew** **fills** **disk** on **streams** **node**.

sections.title: Interview Drill

conceptual.question: What is a **topology**?
conceptual.answer: Directed graph of **processors** (sources, sinks, stateful ops) compiled from **StreamsBuilder**. Explain **second-order** effects: **retry** amplification, **queue** growth, **thread** **pool** exhaustion, and **cost** of extra **hops** when **edge** dependencies like **Redis** rate-limit stores fail. Close with **prevention**: **contract** tests for **clients**, **SLO** alerts on **client-side** **error** rates, **game** days that **kill** **pods** during **business** hours in **staging**, and **lint** rules banning **blocking** calls on **reactor** threads.

followUps.question: What production metric would you watch first to validate that answer?
followUps.answer: For **What is a **topology**?**, pair **consumer lag per partition**, **under-replicated partitions**, and **request/queue time on brokers** with **traces** from producing services. Alert on sustained lag growth and ISR shrinkage, not only error rates.

followUps.question: Which configuration or cluster policy most often invalidates a textbook answer?
followUps.answer: **min.insync.replicas** vs **replication.factor**, **unclean leader election**, **retention/compaction** on money topics, and **max.poll.interval.ms** vs handler runtime usually surface gaps between slides and production.

conceptual.question: **KStream** vs **KTable**?
conceptual.answer: **KStream** is **record** stream; **KTable** is **changelog**-backed **key-value** **materialization**. Mention **verification**: **`curl -v`** through **gateway**, **`kubectl logs`**, **APM** **waterfall** **spans**, and **dashboards** that slice **p99** by **downstream** **service** **name**. Senior answers separate **symptom** from **root** **cause**: **502** at **edge** can be **empty** **Endpoints**, **stale** **pool**, or **upstream** **panic**—each needs different **first** **commands**.

followUps.question: What production metric would you watch first to validate that answer?
followUps.answer: For ****KStream** vs **KTable**?**, pair **consumer lag per partition**, **under-replicated partitions**, and **request/queue time on brokers** with **traces** from producing services. Alert on sustained lag growth and ISR shrinkage, not only error rates.

followUps.answer: **min.insync.replicas** vs **replication.factor**, **unclean leader election**, **retention/compaction** on money topics, and **max.poll.interval.ms** vs handler runtime usually surface gaps between slides and production.

conceptual.question: What is **changelog** topic?
conceptual.answer: Durable **log** backing **state stores** for **fault** tolerance and **restore**. Communicate **executive**-friendly **impact**: **customer** **minutes** degraded, **error** **budget** burn, **mitigation** **ETA**, and **post-incident** **themes** mapped to **roadmap** work. Reference **security**: **mTLS** **service** identity, **OAuth2** **scopes**, **audit** logs at **edge**, and why **secrets** never belong in **Feign** **URL** query strings.

followUps.question: What production metric would you watch first to validate that answer?
followUps.answer: For **What is **changelog** topic?**, pair **consumer lag per partition**, **under-replicated partitions**, and **request/queue time on brokers** with **traces** from producing services. Alert on sustained lag growth and ISR shrinkage, not only error rates.

conceptual.question: **Windowed** operations?
conceptual.answer: **Time-based** **grouping** with **grace** period for **lateness** handling. When **Kubernetes** is involved, cite **readiness**, **HPA**, **NetworkPolicy**, and **Service** **DNS** as factors that change **latency** independent of **Java** **code**. For **gateway** and **service** stacks, emphasize **timeout** ordering (**outer > inner**), **bulkheads** per **tenant**, and **JWT** **cache** **TTL** during **issuer** rotations.

followUps.question: What production metric would you watch first to validate that answer?

conceptual.answer: Expose **local** **state** **stores** for **read** **side** serving with **metadata** routing. For **gateway** and **service** stacks, emphasize **timeout** ordering (**outer > inner**), **bulkheads** per **tenant**, and **JWT** **cache** **TTL** during **issuer** rotations. conceptual.answer: **Time**-driven **callbacks** for periodic maintenance or **emitting** derived outputs. Interviewers reward naming **metrics** (`http_client_requests_seconds`, `resilience4j_circuitbreaker_state`), **trace** correlation IDs, and **rollback** criteria rather than hand-wavy resilience claims. conceptual.question: What makes **cross-AZ Kafka** expensive beyond broker CPU?
conceptual.answer: **Replication** and **fetch** traffic across AZs can dominate **egress** bills. **Rack awareness** and **replica placement** reduce correlated loss and unnecessary **cross-zone** fan-out—finance and platform should review topology together.

conceptual.question: Why is **exactly-once** a **system** property, not a broker toggle?
conceptual.answer: You must align **producer** settings, **consumer** processing, **sink** idempotency, and often **transactions** or **outbox** patterns. Claiming EOS without covering **side effects** is a common **interview** failure mode.

followUps.question: What failure mode shows up first when this is misconfigured under load?
followUps.answer: Typical symptoms: **rebalance storms**, **duplicate delivery** after retries, **metadata refresh loops** (**NOT_LEADER**), or **producer buffer exhaustion**. Tie **// KStream<String,String> s = builder.stream("orders");** to observable signals before jumping to code changes.

followUps.question: How would you prove correctness in staging without flaky integration tests?
followUps.answer: Use **deterministic** workloads: fixed keys, controlled partition counts, inject broker pause/leader movement in a test cluster, and assert **offset** progress + **exactly-once** expectations with idempotent sinks or test harnesses.

followUps.answer: Typical symptoms: **rebalance storms**, **duplicate delivery** after retries, **metadata refresh loops** (**NOT_LEADER**), or **producer buffer exhaustion**. Tie **// replicated table for broadcast lookups** to observable signals before jumping to code changes.

codeBased.question: Why might **Avro** **GenericRecord** be slower than **SpecificRecord**?
codeBased.answer: **Specific** code generation avoids **schema** resolution overhead and enables **stronger** typing in **hot** paths. **Generic** is flexible but pays **reflection-like** costs—measure before micro-optimizing.

followUps.question: What failure mode shows up first when this is misconfigured under load?
followUps.answer: Typical symptoms: **rebalance storms**, **duplicate delivery** after retries, **metadata refresh loops** (**NOT_LEADER**), or **producer buffer exhaustion**. Tie ****Specific** code generation avoids **schema** resolution overhead and enables **stronger** typing i** to observable signals before jumping to code changes.

followUps.answer: Use **deterministic** workloads: fixed keys, controlled partition counts, inject broker pause/leader movement in a test cluster, and assert **offset** progress + **exactly-once** expectations with idempotent sinks or test harnesses.

codeBased.question: What does `isolation.level=read_committed` change for a consumer?
codeBased.answer: It hides **uncommitted** **transactional** messages until commit, aligning reads with **transaction** boundaries. **Latency** can increase; use only when **transactions** are in play.

followUps.question: What failure mode shows up first when this is misconfigured under load?

Tie **It hides **uncommitted** **transactional** messages until commit, aligning reads with **transaction*** to observable signals before jumping to code changes.

seniorScenario.question: **Incident:** **Restore** time hours after **broker** outage.
seniorScenario.answer:
**(1) Immediate response** Check **changelog** retention.

**(2) Root cause** **Retention** shorter than **restore** gap.

**(3) Fix** Raise retention or **standby** replicas.

**(4) Prevention** Synthetic **kill** task drill. When **Kubernetes** is involved, cite **readiness**, **HPA**, **NetworkPolicy**, and **Service** **DNS** as factors that change **latency** independent of **Java** **code**. For **gateway** and **service** stacks, emphasize **timeout** ordering (**outer > inner**), **bulkheads** per **tenant**, and **JWT** **cache** **TTL** during **issuer** rotations. Communicate **executive**-friendly **impact**: **customer** **minutes** degraded, **error** **budget** burn, **mitigation** **ETA**, and **post-incident** **themes** mapped to **roadmap** work. Reference **security**: **mTLS** **service** identity, **OAuth2** **scopes**, **audit** logs at **edge**, and why **secrets** never belong in **Feign** **URL** query strings. Contrast **happy** **path** docs with **partition** behavior: **split** **retries**, **half-open** **probes**, **degraded** **read** modes, and **idempotency** keys that prevent **duplicate** **writes**. Mention **verification**: **`curl -v`** through **gateway**, **`kubectl logs`**, **APM** **waterfall** **spans**, and **dashboards** that slice **p99** by **downstream** **service** **name**. Senior answers separate **symptom** from **root** **cause**: **502** at **edge** can be **empty** **Endpoints**, **stale** **pool**, or **upstream** **panic**—each needs different **first** **commands**.

followUps.question: How do you communicate blast radius and ETA to stakeholders during the incident?
followUps.answer: For ****Incident:** **Restore** time hours after **broker** outage.**, report **which topics/partitions** lag, **whether writes are still accepted**, **data-loss risk** (ISR), and **consumer backlog time**. Give a **bounded** ETA tied to a concrete mitigation (scale, pause traffic, fail over).

followUps.question: What permanent guardrails prevent a repeat in six months?
followUps.answer: Add **SLOs** on lag and ISR, **game days** for AZ loss and broker restarts, **capacity models** for partitions, and **review gates** for retention/compaction and ACL changes.

seniorScenario.answer:
**(1) Immediate response** Key audit.

**(2) Root cause** Keys not co-partitioned.

**(3) Fix** Fix **partition count** + **key** strategy.

**(4) Prevention** Contract tests on join outputs. seniorScenario.question: **Ops:** Disk 95% on **state.dir**.
seniorScenario.answer:
**(1) Immediate response** Stop-the-world risk.

**(2) Root cause** RocksDB **growth** unbounded.

**(3) Fix** Right-size disk + **standby** + compact state.

**(4) Prevention** Alert early. Contrast **happy** **path** docs with **partition** behavior: **split** **retries**, **half-open** **probes**, **degraded** **read** modes, and **idempotency** keys that prevent **duplicate** **writes**. Mention **verification**: **`curl -v`** through **gateway**, **`kubectl logs`**, **APM** **waterfall** **spans**, and **dashboards** that slice **p99** by **downstream** **service** **name**. Senior answers separate **symptom** from **root** **cause**: **502** at **edge** can be **empty** **Endpoints**, **stale** **pool**, or **upstream** **panic**—each needs different **first** **commands**. Call out **ownership**: **platform** **SRE** for **mesh**/**certs**, **service** teams for **timeout** policy, **SRE** for **SLO** **burn** **alerts**—and how those teams **coordinate** during **sev-1** calls. Interviewers reward naming **metrics** (`http_client_requests_seconds`, `resilience4j_circuitbreaker_state`), **trace** correlation IDs, and **rollback** criteria rather than hand-wavy resilience claims. Tie answers to **Spring** reality: **Feign** + **LoadBalancer**, **WebClient** + **`ExchangeFilterFunction`**, **Resilience4j** registries, and **Micrometer** tags that make **on-call** searches fast.

followUps.question: How do you communicate blast radius and ETA to stakeholders during the incident?

seniorScenario.answer:
**(1) Immediate response** Challenge requirements.

**(2) Root cause** Infinite grace impossible.

**(3) Fix** Separate **late lane** topic + **approx** window.

**(4) Prevention** SLA doc. For **gateway** and **service** stacks, emphasize **timeout** ordering (**outer > inner**), **bulkheads** per **tenant**, and **JWT** **cache** **TTL** during **issuer** rotations. followUps.answer: Add **SLOs** on lag and ISR, **game days** for AZ loss and broker restarts, **capacity models** for partitions, and **review gates** for retention/compaction and ACL changes.

seniorScenario.question: **Scale:** **Single** **thread** pegs CPU on heavy **aggregate**.
seniorScenario.answer:
**(1) Immediate response** Profile hot store.

**(2) Root cause** Key explosion.

**(3) Fix** Salting for analytics or pre-aggregation upstream.

**(4) Prevention** Capacity model. Call out **ownership**: **platform** **SRE** for **mesh**/**certs**, **service** teams for **timeout** policy, **SRE** for **SLO** **burn** **alerts**—and how those teams **coordinate** during **sev-1** calls. Interviewers reward naming **metrics** (`http_client_requests_seconds`, `resilience4j_circuitbreaker_state`), **trace** correlation IDs, and **rollback** criteria rather than hand-wavy resilience claims. Tie answers to **Spring** reality: **Feign** + **LoadBalancer**, **WebClient** + **`ExchangeFilterFunction`**, **Resilience4j** registries, and **Micrometer** tags that make **on-call** searches fast. Explain **second-order** effects: **retry** amplification, **queue** growth, **thread** **pool** exhaustion, and **cost** of extra **hops** when **edge** dependencies like **Redis** rate-limit stores fail. Close with **prevention**: **contract** tests for **clients**, **SLO** alerts on **client-side** **error** rates, **game** days that **kill** **pods** during **business** hours in **staging**, and **lint** rules banning **blocking** calls on **reactor** threads. When **Kubernetes** is involved, cite **readiness**, **HPA**, **NetworkPolicy**, and **Service** **DNS** as factors that change **latency** independent of **Java** **code**.

seniorScenario.answer:
**(1) Immediate response** Freeze change.

**(2) Root cause** Abort storm risk.

**(3) Fix** Blue/green apps with feature flag.

**(4) Prevention** Runbook validation. When **Kubernetes** is involved, cite **readiness**, **HPA**, **NetworkPolicy**, and **Service** **DNS** as factors that change **latency** independent of **Java** **code**. followUps.answer: For ****Migration:** **EOS** toggle during peak.**, report **which topics/partitions** lag, **whether writes are still accepted**, **data-loss risk** (ISR), and **consumer backlog time**. Give a **bounded** ETA tied to a concrete mitigation (scale, pause traffic, fail over).

wrongAnswers: **Streams** removes need for **topics** — **Correction:** **Internal** topics still exist.
**Join** works without **same** partitions — **Correction:** **Repartition** required unless already aligned.
**Suppress** fixes **ordering** globally — **Correction:** Partition ordering still applies.
**RocksDB** needs no monitoring — **Correction:** **Disk** and **compaction** pressure matter.
**Streams** is only for JVM batch — **Correction:** Continuous streaming model.
**grace=0** is always best — **Correction:** Late data dropped unexpectedly.

sections.title: MCQ Quiz — Kafka Streams
sections.description: Thirty questions from basic to advanced — Kafka Streams. Read every option; distractors are plausible but wrong for a precise reason.

questions.question: [Kafka Streams] Which statement best describes **topology** in production Kafka/messaging contexts?

options.A: It is a core lever teams tune with metrics, failure modes, and SLOs in mind.
options.B: It is purely a UI concern unrelated to brokers or clients.
options.C: It disables all replication automatically.
options.D: It guarantees global total order for every topic.

questions.explanation: **topology** belongs to the operational model for **Kafka Streams**—tie it to trade-offs and measurements, not absolutes.

questions.question: [Kafka Streams] Which option is a **plausible distractor** interviewers use about **KTable vs KStream**?

options.A: A precise, bounded statement tied to ordering, ISR, or consumer groups.
options.B: A blanket claim that ignores partition boundaries or client semantics.
options.C: A metric-backed troubleshooting step.
options.D: A configuration paired with a failure mode.

questions.explanation: Watch for absolutes on **KTable vs KStream** that ignore **per-partition** semantics and client responsibilities.

questions.question: [Kafka Streams] Incident: user-visible delay worsens after a deploy touching **state store**. What is a credible first hypothesis?

options.A: The deploy changed timing, batching, retries, or resource limits along the critical path.
options.B: The system randomly deleted all state.
options.C: DNS always causes delay; ignore application logic.
options.D: You always need fewer threads when latency grows.

questions.explanation: Tie **state store** to measurable regressions: compare golden signals before/after and narrow to the changed surface.

questions.explanation: **changelog topic** belongs to the operational model for **Kafka Streams**—tie it to trade-offs and measurements, not absolutes.

questions.question: [Kafka Streams] You see misconfiguration around **rocksdb** in a code review. What is the best first response?

options.A: Map settings to infrastructure policy, then reproduce under load in a controlled environment.

options.C: Set all timeouts to 1ms for safety.
options.D: Disable metrics to reduce noise.

questions.explanation: Validate **rocksdb** against defaults, workload, and symptoms (lag, errors, consistency) before changing code.

options.D: You always need fewer threads when latency grows.

questions.explanation: Tie **changelog topic** to measurable regressions: compare golden signals before/after and narrow to the changed surface.

questions.question: [Kafka Streams] Which statement best describes **rocksdb** in production Kafka/messaging contexts?

options.A: It is a core lever teams tune with metrics, failure modes, and SLOs in mind.
options.B: It is purely a UI concern unrelated to brokers or clients.
options.C: It disables all replication automatically.
options.D: It guarantees global total order for every topic.

questions.explanation: **rocksdb** belongs to the operational model for **Kafka Streams**—tie it to trade-offs and measurements, not absolutes.

questions.question: [Kafka Streams] You see misconfiguration around **windowing** in a code review. What is the best first response?

options.A: Map settings to infrastructure policy, then reproduce under load in a controlled environment.
options.B: Delete the topic and recreate it with fewer partitions.
options.C: Set all timeouts to 1ms for safety.
options.D: Disable metrics to reduce noise.

questions.explanation: Validate **windowing** against defaults, workload, and symptoms (lag, errors, consistency) before changing code.

questions.question: [Kafka Streams] Incident: user-visible delay worsens after a deploy touching **join**. What is a credible first hypothesis?

options.A: The deploy changed timing, batching, retries, or resource limits along the critical path.
options.B: The system randomly deleted all state.
options.C: DNS always causes delay; ignore application logic.
options.D: You always need fewer threads when latency grows.

questions.explanation: Tie **join** to measurable regressions: compare golden signals before/after and narrow to the changed surface.

questions.question: [Kafka Streams] Which statement best describes **repartition topic** in production Kafka/messaging contexts?

options.A: It is a core lever teams tune with metrics, failure modes, and SLOs in mind.
options.B: It is purely a UI concern unrelated to brokers or clients.
options.C: It disables all replication automatically.
options.D: It guarantees global total order for every topic.

questions.explanation: **repartition topic** belongs to the operational model for **Kafka Streams**—tie it to trade-offs and measurements, not absolutes.

questions.question: [Kafka Streams] You see misconfiguration around **task assignment** in a code review. What is the best first response?

options.A: Map settings to infrastructure policy, then reproduce under load in a controlled environment.
options.B: Delete the topic and recreate it with fewer partitions.
options.C: Set all timeouts to 1ms for safety.
options.D: Disable metrics to reduce noise.

questions.explanation: Validate **task assignment** against defaults, workload, and symptoms (lag, errors, consistency) before changing code.

questions.question: [Kafka Streams] Which option is a **plausible distractor** interviewers use about **standby replica**?

options.A: A precise, bounded statement tied to ordering, ISR, or consumer groups.
options.B: A blanket claim that ignores partition boundaries or client semantics.
options.C: A metric-backed troubleshooting step.
options.D: A configuration paired with a failure mode.

questions.explanation: Watch for absolutes on **standby replica** that ignore **per-partition** semantics and client responsibilities.

sections.title: Kafka Streams — cheatsheet
sections.content:
| Topic | Rule of thumb | Interview one-liner |
| --- | --- | --- |
| topology | Processor graph | Processor graph |
| changelog | Durable state log | Durable state log |
| RocksDB | Local KV persistence | Local KV persistence |
| repartition | Shuffle via internal topic | Shuffle via internal topic |
| grace | Late record tolerance | Late record tolerance |
| IQ | Serve local state | Serve local state |
| EOS mode | Broker + client pairing | Broker + client pairing |
| standby | Faster failover | Faster failover |
| co-partition | Join prerequisite | Join prerequisite |
| suppress | Reduce output churn | Reduce output churn |
| Operational metric | Watch lag + ISR + request latency | Say lag per partition and under-replicated partitions |
| Failure drill | Broker bounce + consumer crash chaos | Prove rebalance and recovery SLOs in staging |
| Capacity | Partitions bound parallelism per group | More consumers than partitions stay idle |
| Security | ACLs least privilege per principal | Separate prod cluster or strict topic governance |
| Cost | Retention drives disk and replay cost | Tiered storage or export before cutting retention |
